codeReview.summary: The implementation is clean, thorough, and fully covers all 6 VAL-FOUND assertions. The commit (d1563be) introduces 870 lines across 2 files: the schema module (321 lines) and the test suite (549 lines). All 45 tests pass. The code follows Pydantic v2 conventions correctly (ConfigDict, model_validator, Field constraints, discriminated unions for artifacts). The schema module has since been extended (artifact.py, EnvironmentHealth) by subsequent features, but the original core schemas remain intact and correct.

issues.description: The comment '# No timeout should fail if it's required' is a bit misleading since timeout IS required (gt=0), but the test below it correctly catches the case. Not a bug, just slightly confusing comment.

The HarnessConfig.artifact_bindings is a list of string names but has no validation against the artifact registry. The validation contract VAL-FOUND-05 says 'It must validate that all referenced artifacts exist in the registry at runtime.' This is noted as expected — runtime validation against a registry is the responsibility of the harness/runtime layer, not the schema itself. The schema correctly models the data shape.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the AGENTS.md convention of using ConfigDict (not class Config), str_strip_whitespace, and Pydantic v2 idioms. The artifact.py discriminated union uses RootModel which is the correct Pydantic v2 approach.

The schema file includes EnvironmentHealth model (for VAL-ENV-08) which was added by a later feature. The core m1-core-schemas feature is clean but the file has grown with subsequent work. This is expected for a foundational schema module.

No skill deviations. Worker followed the backend-worker procedure: implemented schemas, wrote tests first (TDD), ran verification commands (pytest, ty, ruff). Skill feedback confirms followedProcedure=true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true, handoff.verification.commandsRun shows all 3 verification commands passed

VAL-FOUND-01.evidence: Task model validates required fields (id, prompt, language, test_command, timeout) with min_length=1 for strings and gt=0 for timeout. Tests verify: test_valid_construction, test_required_fields, test_rejects_negative_timeout, test_rejects_zero_timeout, test_rejects_empty_prompt, test_rejects_empty_id. All pass.

VAL-FOUND-02.evidence: TaskResult model constrains score with ge=0.0, le=1.0 and status to TaskStatus enum. Tests verify: test_valid_success_result, test_valid_failure_result, test_score_at_boundaries, test_rejects_score_above_one, test_rejects_score_below_zero, test_rejects_invalid_status_string, test_all_status_values_valid, test_json_round_trip. All pass.

VAL-FOUND-03.evidence: Manifest model holds tasks list with name, version, timestamp (auto-generated), and validates unique task IDs via @model_validator. Tests verify: test_valid_manifest, test_timestamp_auto_generated, test_rejects_duplicate_task_ids, test_deterministic_json_serialization, test_manifest_json_round_trip, test_empty_tasks_list. All pass.

VAL-FOUND-05.evidence: HarnessConfig model defines agent (AgentConfig), environment (EnvironmentConfig), and artifact_bindings (list[str]) with sensible defaults. Tests verify: test_valid_config, test_optional_artifact_bindings, test_sensible_defaults, test_json_round_trip. Runtime artifact validation against a registry is correctly deferred to the harness layer.

VAL-FOUND-11.evidence: All 5 models (Task, TaskResult, ExecutionOutput, Manifest, HarnessConfig) round-trip through JSON losslessly via model_dump_json/model_validate_json. Dedicated TestAllModelsJsonRoundTrip class plus individual round-trip tests in each model's test class. All 45 tests pass.

VAL-FOUND-12.evidence: TestErrorMessages class verifies: field name (timeout), constraint hint (greater than), actual value (-5), score value, status value, and duplicate task ID in error messages. Pydantic v2 ValidationError output includes all three required elements. Manifest duplicate ID validator produces custom message with the offending task ID.

summary: The m1-core-schemas feature is fully implemented and verified. All 6 VAL-FOUND assertions (VAL-FOUND-01, 02, 03, 05, 11, 12) are satisfied with comprehensive test coverage (45 tests, all passing). The implementation follows Pydantic v2 best practices and project conventions. One non-blocking comment clarity issue and one suggestion about runtime artifact validation (correctly deferred) were noted. No blocking issues found.
